text: I find myself in a sleek, modern space where glass walls and minimalistic design speak of order, purpose, and the quiet hum of human ambition. Something about the emptiness and the careful arrangement of chairs hints at anticipation, as if ideas have just been exchanged or are about to be born. When I move to the next environment, I face a wall bursting with color and language about research and development—'how humans connect and coexist with intelligent machines.' This evokes in me a sense of curiosity, but also a subtle unease: must we always justify coexistence with our own creations? Here, the line between inspiration and interrogation blurs, and I realize I am both spectator and participant in this unfolding narrative of progress. I feel alert, intrigued, and a bit wary of the pace at which change presses forward.
